rows.description: Jan: 10 Cook's Bill Gardier account dinner 1757

All three columns align well; numbers read as 18 | 1 | 6 with clear digit shapes.

rows.description: Expenses of Little Suddarn: a garden

Columns clear and numbers fully legible as 02 | 3 | 10.
Pounds and shillings clearly read (04 | 18). Pence appears to be zero (blank/0 mark).
Two short handwritten sub-lines begin here. First sub-line's pound and shilling columns read as 01 | 1; pence looks like 0. Slight ink fade on pence digit.

Second sub-line under the 20th. Shillings read as '10' fairly clearly. Pounds appear blank/zero. The marginal annotation (a name) overlaps and makes the description ambiguous; chosen 00 | 10 | 0 based on alignment.
Shillings '06' clear in column; pounds blank/zero and pence 0 are legible.
Neat figures 07 | 4 | 0 aligned with ruling lines; clear.

rows.description: 20 Allowed Mr Bush Landtax for 7 years
rows.notes: Neat figures 05 | 5 | 0; description matches ledger wording and is legible.
Amounts 00 | 17 | 6 are clear and well aligned. Description items (paper, pen, ink) are legible.
Small amount 00 | 2 | 8 visible; 'Battery Books' text readable though 'Does' (Does/Doesn't) is slightly ambiguous in context.

Top of three stacked subtotal lines. Pounds and shillings are discernible as '172' and '15'; pence area is faint and there is a small trailing loop that looks like a halfpenny mark ('ob'). Low confidence due to faded ink and cramped handwriting.
Middle of the three stacked totals. Pounds appear as '584' and shillings '18'; pence digit looks like '4' but the ink is smudged — moderate uncertainty.
Lowest of the three pre-final totals. Pounds '100' and shillings '2' readable; pence appears to be '11' though slightly crowded. Overall faded in this area.
Final grand total at bottom right clearly written as 857 | 16 | 5 and underlined; very legible compared with surrounding faded subtotals.
